Local fuel dealers are loath to
believe that the railroad cOmpan-
les upon which Spokane depends
for its coal supply are deliberately
holding back the output of mines
they own or control in order to
cinch the consumer on famine
prices when cold weather begins.
They admit that the possibility as
set forth in The Press is plausible
and possible, but hesitate to &C
cept the idea that wholesale rob-
berry by that means would be at
tempted by railroad management.
They hold this faith In spite Of the
disclosures of combination methods
of bleeding exposed and now made
difficult and dangerous further
east. The suggestion follows here
that, driven out of old pastures, the
corporation holdup has taken
Greeley's advice and come west,
where illegal profit, while smaller,
may be more safely filched.


However, no direct accusation
can be made against either the
Northern Pacific or Union Pacific
fixing them the charge of con-
spiracy in connection with the ex-
treme shortage of fuel all through
the Northwest. AS the situation
lies, the roads would seem to be
safe enough had they deliberately
planned gigantic holdup. 1t
would reuire more potent prose-
cution than this part of the coun-
try has yet known anything about
to prove 1t.


But here Is one reason for the
prevailing scarcity of rolling stock
that railroad men cannot deny and
excuse. OF late years the profits
of nearly all the big lines, notably
the Hill roads, have been put into
dividends instead of improve
